title: Paths, Navigation, and Filesystem Hierarchy · Terminal Trace: Absolute Relative Paths
summary: Locate application configuration and logs on a workstation whose current directory and distribution conventions differ from a familiar machine. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux absolute relative paths increment in a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.
Record command, expected signal, actual output, exit status, interpretation, and next decision as separate evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: On a worked example host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory. A defensible solution applies Absolute Relative Paths, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-absolute-relative-paths — Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.
pwd
printf 'shell=%s\n' "$BASH_VERSION"
type -a printf
command -V pwd
printf 'status=%d\n' "$?"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
